name: Pac-Man
description:
Pac-Man is a maze-based chase game. The player controls a yellow, spherical character, Pac-Man, within an enclosed maze. The maze contains numerous small white pellets, four colored ghosts (Blinky, Pinky, Inky, and Clyde), and four larger, flashing "power pellets" located in the corners of the maze. Pac-Man's primary objective is to consume all of the pellets in the maze while avoiding contact with the ghosts. 

The ghosts continuously patrol the maze, each with distinct behavioral patterns. Contact with a ghost results in the loss of a life. The game ends when Pac-Man loses all lives. 

Consuming a power pellet temporarily reverses the roles of Pac-Man and the ghosts. During this period, the ghosts turn blue and become vulnerable. Pac-Man can eat the blue ghosts for bonus points. The power pellet effect is temporary, and the ghosts eventually revert to their normal, dangerous state. After being eaten, ghosts return to a central "ghost house" area to regenerate and eventually re-enter the maze. 

The maze may also contain fruit or other bonus items that appear periodically, which Pac-Man can consume for additional points. After all pellets are eaten a new level is loaded that has the same layout but starts with all pellets in place. The difficulty increases gradually with each level.
